description: ol-duplicate-binder (issue #440), no double-report: in `reduce x [:x :x] in …` the name `x` both collides with the accumulator (#407/F8) AND repeats inside the pattern. It must raise exactly ONE `ol-duplicate-binder`, not two — the pattern-internal repeat already points at the later occurrence, so the accumulator collision for that same name is suppressed. The single finding is `form:"destructuring"` at the second `:x`. Contrast `duplicate-binder-reduce-pattern-accumulator` (`reduce x [:x :y]`), where the collided name is not repeated and the finding stays `form:"reduce"`. `:pairs` is declared first so no `ol-undefined-var` fires.
